fields.description:
## Why
Production-severity axes + the mean structurally mis-measure code-review's maintainability/latent-regression
findings: a feature silently dead in prod (untested wiring) scores 0.415 because prod_impact is 'none' and
reversibility 'easy' drag it down. Landmines and nits overlap so no scalar threshold separates them.

## What
A code-review-specific, verifier-embedded impact model dispatched per-gate off `decide.py`.
- TIER VALUES: each consequence binary carries the severity it implies if true - minor=0.3, moderate=0.6,
  serious=0.9. Lane-severity = MAX of the tiers of the TRUE binaries (no dilution; a minor/moderate binary
  cannot alone reach the block zone). No cross-binary compounding (conservative default).
- TWO LANES: prod_lane = MAX(correctness tier-tagged binaries) * likelihood (common=1.0/sometimes=0.6/
  rare=0.25); maint_lane = MAX(maintainability tier-tagged binaries) * freq_mult.
- CHANGE-FREQUENCY MULTIPLIER (explicit formula): `freq_mult = 0.5 + 0.5 * min(churn_90d, 30) / 30` where
  churn_90d = number of commits touching the file in the last 90 days (from `git log`, DET); linear, saturates
  at 30. Applies to maint_lane only.
- REVERSIBILITY FLOOR (det/hybrid): if the change touches a hard-to-reverse surface (migration dir, exported
  public API, persisted/serialization format, deletion, released artifact), `reversibility_floor = 0.6`, else 0.
- DETECTION AMPLIFIER: `amp = 1.0 if (silent_failure or escapes_automation) else 0.8`.
- EXPLICIT COMPOSE ORDER (single expression, no ambiguity):
  `impact_base = MAX(prod_lane, maint_lane); impact = max(min(1.0, impact_base * amp), reversibility_floor)`.
- RE-ANCHOR: drop the 0.33 attribute floor; co-calibrate tier values + block threshold on the labeled set
  (below) so a maintainability landmine reaches ~0.8+ and a nit ~0.15-0.2.
- CARVED SUB-QUESTION SET (audit-approved; each serious binary carries its charitable carve-out): serious =
  {data-loss-without-recovery, security-bypass-not-enforced-elsewhere, silent-wrong-feeding-a-decision,
  unversioned-published-contract-break, safety-net-removal-without-replacement}; moderate/minor = {capability-
  degraded, contract-drift, hidden-invariant, implicit-coupling, dead-code}.

## Implementation mechanics + calibration data
- SUB-ANSWER SCHEMA: emit the tier-tagged binaries as new fields inside the SINGLE aggregate Pass-2 verify
  structured output (a `code_review_verification_model()` extending the shared model), alongside the existing
  severity_attributes - no added LLM calls. Reversibility/reach/change-frequency are DET/hybrid (diff + git).
- PER-GATE DISPATCH: use the same `impact_fn` param added to `pass3_decide()` (default = existing `impact`);
  the code-review wrapper passes `impact_fn=impact_code`. Plan-review is byte-unchanged (asserted by test).
- CALIBRATION/LABELED SET: a curated fixture `tests/unit/fixtures/code_review_impact_labels.jsonl` of
  code-review findings each tagged HIGH (block-worthy) or NIT, provenance = the dogfooded code-review corpus +
  this program's held-out adjudication (initial ~31 labeled findings; grows via the data-capture corpus). Used
  for the re-anchoring co-calibration and the AC5 diff-grounded A/B.
- ADR: this novel two-lane model is an architectural decision - record it under `docs/adr/`.

## Scope
- `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py` (`impact_code` via `impact_fn`), `verify.py`
  (`code_review_verification_model`), the code-review verify prompt, the DET signals, the labeled fixture +
  re-anchoring calibration, and the ADR. Plan-review impact unchanged.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `impact_code = max(min(1.0, MAX(prod_lane, maint_lane) * amp), reversibility_floor)` per the explicit
      compose above, with tier-tagged-binary MAX within each lane using the stated tier values and the stated
      freq_mult / reversibility_floor / amp formulas.
- [ ] Carved serious/moderate/minor sub-question set implemented with charitable carve-outs; emitted in ONE
      aggregate Pass-2 call (no added LLM calls); reversibility/reach/change-frequency are DET/hybrid.
- [ ] Per-gate dispatch via `impact_fn`; plan-review byte-unchanged (asserted by test); an ADR records the model.
- [ ] The `code_review_impact_labels.jsonl` fixture exists (HIGH/NIT, provenance documented); simulation on it
      shows the model separates HIGH from NIT better than the old scalar (or the gap is explained), validated
      diff-grounded before any threshold-down tuning.
